role_delineation_check: SW manages HHA referral selection and clinical discharge planning for the CHF transition. PN verifies demographic accuracy in Atlantis, educates the family on the 24-hour visit window, and flags caregiver sentiment concerns to the SW.
demographic_audit_note: Audit of Face Sheet against Atlantis revealed a mismatch in the primary contact number; Face Sheet listed a disconnected landline. Updated Atlantis with daughter’s (POA) mobile number and verified Medicare Advantage eligibility.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with HHA (Goal confirmed by SW during Stage 1 Intake).
weekly_staff_update: Week 2: Patient clinically stable on IV diuretics; SW reports HHA referral sent. Daughter (caregiver) expresses rising anxiety regarding daily weight monitoring and sodium restrictions.
v_card_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into daughter's phone for Caller ID recognition; program flyer delivered and Day 1 expectations reviewed.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to D/C. Daughter in 'panic' state, threatening to refuse discharge because she 'cannot handle the CHF equipment.' Sentiment verified as 'Anxious/Reluctant'.
atlantis_final_sync: Confirmed D/C date of 11/04 and 1st visit date of 11/05 entered into Atlantis after HHA portal showed 'Accepted'.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit scheduled for 11/05 at 11:00 AM. Booking occurred ONLY after SW confirmed HHA agency had assigned a nurse for the start-of-care window.
narrative_summary: Initial audit corrected critical contact data in Atlantis, ensuring transition continuity. PN focused on verifying family sentiment as the caregiver transitioned from 'Confident' to 'Panic' regarding CHF home management. PN documented sentiment scores and flagged the Caregiver_Panic barrier to the SW, allowing for a professional care conference before discharge. Final MA visit was booked after confirming HHA readiness via the Atlantis portal.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit identifies stale contact data (disconnected landline).
Daughter expresses total inability to manage patient's CHF scale and medication log.
HHA portal shows 'Pending' status 48 hours before scheduled discharge.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter threatens to block discharge due to fear of CHF monitoring.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented 'Anxious' sentiment score and flagged caregiver panic to the SW.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA status remains unconfirmed in the Atlantis portal.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Asked SW for an update on HHA acceptance status and waited for confirmation.

format_2_triples.situation: Family is unclear on who arrives at the home first.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated family on the 24-hour MA visit timeline and the role of the HHA nurse.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN documents the daughter's verbal refusal to take the patient home and steps back from further education, waiting for the SW to perform a formal 'Safety Pivot' or family mediation.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Strategic deferral allows the SW to handle the clinical/legal implications of a caregiver refusing discharge, respecting the professional boundary.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN verifies the specific technical fears the daughter has regarding CHF monitoring, documents the high-anxiety sentiment in Atlantis, and asks the SW to lead a care conference to address the training gap.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Uses Liaison verbs to surface the barrier (Verify/Document/Ask) and routes the resolution to the SW without contacting vendors or performing clinical assessments.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN calls the HHA Intake Coordinator to demand they send a nurse to the home 4 hours early on Day 1 to 'babysit' the daughter through the first CHF weight check and medication pass.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the No-Vendor Rule and involves the PN in clinical/operational coordination that belongs to the HHA and SW.
